Sales.broad: sales-slack-2026-01-08: Big news — three Q4 deals that didn't close before Christmas have landed in January. Redwood, Apex, and Fortis all signed this week. Combined first-month billing £3,200. The January budget was set at £500 assuming a quiet month — looks like the pipeline catch-up exceeded expectations.
Sales.narrow: sales-slack-2026-01-15: Sales reconciliation done. Total January bookings £5,621: Redwood £2,100, Apex £1,850, Fortis £1,300, two smaller inbound leads £371. All revenue recognised in January.

Sales.broad: gmail-2026-01-08-pipeline: Q4 pipeline carry-over memo from CEO — three major deals (Redwood, Apex, Fortis) that were forecast to close by 31 December 2025 have now signed in January. Revenue recognition approved by CFO. January budget variance is expected and approved.
Sales.narrow: gmail-2026-01-15-rev-sign-off: CFO sign-off on January revenue: £5,621 recognised. January plan was £500 (conservative placeholder for traditionally slow month). Variance is fully explained by Q4 carry-over.

Sales.broad: cal-2026-01-07: Pipeline review — CEO and CFO 1:1 to discuss Q4 deals that missed December close. Three deals confirmed for January signing.
Sales.narrow: cal-2026-01-08: Contract signing day — Redwood (10:00), Apex (14:00), Fortis (16:30). All virtual.

Sales.broad: crm-q4-carryover: CRM pipeline report January 2026. Redwood Group — Closed Won 8 Jan (ARR £2,100). Apex Analytics — Closed Won 8 Jan (ARR £1,850). Fortis Ltd — Closed Won 9 Jan (ARR £1,300). Total carry-over from Q4 2025: £5,250.
Sales.narrow: crm-jan-additional: Two additional inbound deals closed Jan 12–15: Crest Digital £220, BlueSky Ops £151. Total January pipeline recognition £5,621.
